Title: OpenSSL Multiple Vulnerabilities
Threat: A buffer overflow was found in the X.509 certificate verification code in OpenSSL 3.0.x prior to 3.0.7.
Solution: Upgrade OpenSSL to 3.0.7 or later.

Title: Apache HTTP Server Multiple Vulnerabilities
Threat: Multiple vulnerabilities affecting Apache HTTP Server prior to 2.4.54 including path traversal.
Solution: Upgrade Apache HTTP Server to 2.4.54 or later.

Title: TLS 1.1 Protocol Supported
Threat: The target accepts TLS 1.1 connections. TLS 1.1 has been deprecated by NIST.
Impact: Data may be decrypted by attackers.
Solution: Disable TLS 1.1 and configure TLS 1.2 or 1.3 only.

Title: SSL Certificate - Signature Verification Failed Policy
Threat: The SSL certificate cannot be trusted because it is self-signed.
Solution: Obtain a certificate from a trusted certificate authority.

Title: Linux sudoers - Heap-Based Buffer Overflow (Baron Samedit)
Threat: A heap-based buffer overflow exists in sudo versions prior to 1.9.5p2 when processing escape characters in sudoers files.
Solution: Upgrade sudo to version 1.9.5p2 or later.

Title: OpenSSH < 9.0 Multiple Vulnerabilities
Threat: The version of OpenSSH running on the remote host is prior to 9.0 and is affected by multiple vulnerabilities.
Impact: Information disclosure and denial of service.
Solution: Upgrade OpenSSH to version 9.0 or later.

Title: SSH Weak Message Authentication Code Algorithms
Threat: The SSH server is configured to use HMAC-MD5 or other weak MAC algorithms.
Solution: Configure SSH to use only strong MAC algorithms such as HMAC-SHA2-256.

Title: Missing 'Content-Security-Policy' HTTP Header
Threat: The application does not set a Content-Security-Policy header. This increases risk of XSS attacks.
Solution: Implement a strict Content-Security-Policy header.

Title: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities
Threat: Multiple vulnerabilities in PostgreSQL versions prior to 14.5 / 13.8 / 12.12.
Solution: Upgrade PostgreSQL to the latest supported version.

Title: Linux Kernel Privilege Escalation (Dirty Pipe)
Threat: The Linux kernel versions 5.8 through 5.16.11 allow a local user to overwrite arbitrary files even those marked read-only.
Solution: Update the Linux kernel to a patched version.

Title: NTP Amplification Attack
Threat: The NTP server is configured to respond to monlist queries which can be used as an amplifier in DDoS attacks.
Solution: Disable the monlist feature in ntpd configuration.

Title: SSL/TLS Server Supports RC4 Cipher
Threat: The server supports the RC4 stream cipher which is known to have statistical biases.
Solution: Disable RC4 cipher suite support.

Title: Secure Shell (SSH) Protocol Version 1 Supported
Threat: The remote SSH daemon supports connections made using the version 1 of the SSH protocol.
Impact: Remote code execution and man-in-the-middle attacks.
Solution: Disable SSH protocol version 1 and use version 2 only.

Title: SSH Server Accepts Keyboard-Interactive Authentication
Threat: The remote SSH server allows keyboard-interactive authentication. This can allow brute-force attacks.
Solution: Disable keyboard-interactive authentication and use only public key authentication.

Title: SNMP Agent Default Community Names
Threat: The remote SNMP service uses the default community string 'public'.
Impact: Information disclosure and system configuration changes.
Solution: Change the default SNMP community string to a unique value.

Title: Jenkins Multiple Vulnerabilities
Threat: The Jenkins version installed on the remote host is affected by multiple critical vulnerabilities including a cross-site scripting via a crafted URL and a remote code execution via a crafted request.
Impact: Remote code execution on the Jenkins server.
Solution: Upgrade Jenkins to version 2.375.3 LTS or 2.395 or later.

Title: Docker Registry Unauthenticated Access
Threat: The Docker registry on the remote host does not require authentication. An attacker can pull or push arbitrary container images.
Solution: Configure Docker to require TLS authentication.

Title: Oracle Java Multiple Vulnerabilities
Threat: Multiple vulnerabilities in Oracle Java SE prior to 8u341 / 11.0.16 / 17.0.4.
Solution: Update Java to the latest patched release.

Title: Samba Server Heap Buffer Overflow (CVE-2021-44142)
Threat: The version of Samba installed on the remote host contains a heap-based buffer overflow in the VFS module for Apple filing protocol support.
Impact: Remote code execution as root without authentication.
Solution: Upgrade Samba to 4.13.17 / 4.14.12 / 4.15.5 or later.

Title: NFS Share Exported to the World
Threat: The remote host is exporting NFS shares that can be mounted by any host on the network.
Impact: Unauthorized read/write access to sensitive file data.
Solution: Restrict NFS exports to only trusted hosts.

Title: FTP Anonymous Login Allowed
Threat: The remote host allows anonymous FTP connections. An attacker may use this to retrieve files from or store files on the server.
Solution: Disable anonymous FTP access.

Title: Grafana Path Traversal (CVE-2021-43798)
Threat: The version of Grafana installed on the remote host is 8.x prior to 8.3.2 and is affected by a directory traversal vulnerability.
Solution: Upgrade Grafana to version 8.3.2 or later.

Title: Elasticsearch Unauthenticated Access
Threat: The Elasticsearch instance is accessible without any authentication. All indexed data can be read and modified.
Impact: Full read/write access to search indices and potentially sensitive data.
Solution: Enable Elasticsearch security features and require authentication.

Title: Exim Multiple Vulnerabilities (21Nails)
Threat: The version of Exim installed on the remote host is prior to 4.94.2 and is affected by 21 vulnerabilities collectively known as 21Nails. Several allow remote code execution.
Impact: Remote code execution as root without authentication.
Solution: Upgrade Exim to version 4.94.2 or later.

Title: Open Mail Relay
Threat: The remote SMTP server relays messages to any third-party domain. This can allow spammers to use the server to send bulk email.
Solution: Configure the SMTP server to reject relaying for external senders.
